marketFocus: Trump: 'tremendous success in Iran, they want to make a deal.' Iran gave US a 'significant prize' related to Hormuz. US working on 1-month ceasefire + 15-point plan. Iran still denying formal talks; IRGC hardening posture.
Oil rangebound: WTI $88.50–$92.57/bbl, Brent $100.21–$104.35/bbl. AUD CPI missed: 3.7% y/y (exp 3.8%), Trimmed Mean 0.2% MoM (exp 0.3%). AUD/USD pulled back from 0.7000 resistance.
Flash PMIs: UK Composite 51.0 (miss vs 52.8), EZ Composite 50.5 (miss). Both point to stagflation — war stalling growth while driving inflation. ECB Kazaks: 'two rate hikes seems reasonable.'

USD.tone: Stagflation Signal from PMIs — DXY Rangebound
USD.summary: USD is Neutral today. DXY held a 99.09–99.51 range as Flash PMIs pointed to Q1 GDP of 1.3% while consumer price inflation is seen accelerating to ~4% — a growing stagflation risk. The ceasefire narrative removes the pure risk-off bid but the stagflation signal prevents a clean bullish case. Consumer Confidence (16:00 FFT) is the key remaining event today. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Flash PMIs: Q1 GDP 1.3%, CPI accelerating to ~4% — stagflation signal
DXY rangebound 99.09–99.51; ceasefire hopes cap risk-off bid
Fed Barr: 'rates holding steady for some time; Middle East raises additional risk'
Fed Goolsbee: 'don't know if we can cut again; depends on how long war lasts'
Consumer Confidence at 16:00 FFT — key remaining catalyst today

EUR.tone: PMI Miss + Stagflation Warning — EUR/USD Capped at 21 DMA
EUR.summary: EUR is Bearish today. EUR/USD found resistance at the 21 DMA (1.1617) and is subdued within 1.1575–1.1618. EZ Composite PMI missed at 50.5 (exp 51.0) — 'eurozone GDP slowing to below 0.1% quarterly rate; CPI accelerating close to 3%.' ECB officials (Villeroy, Sleijpen, Kocher, Kazaks) all warned on second-round effects. Kazaks: 'two rate hikes seems reasonable.' German IFO at 10:00 FFT is the next catalyst. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: EZ Composite PMI 50.5 (miss vs 51.0); GDP slowing, CPI accelerating to ~3%
EUR/USD capped at 21 DMA (1.1617); subdued within 1.1575–1.1618
ECB Kazaks: 'two rate hikes seems reasonable; must wait and see'
ECB Sleijpen: 'energy prices likely to become entrenched more quickly than 2022'
German IFO Business Climate at 10:00 FFT — next catalyst

GBP.tone: PMI Miss + BOE Dilemma — GBP/USD Below 200 DMA
GBP.summary: GBP is Bearish today. GBP/USD dipped back under the 200 DMA (1.3434) after UK Composite PMI missed at 51.0 (exp 52.8). 'The war in the Middle East has hit the UK economy in March, stalling growth while driving inflation sharply higher.' UK CPI (07:00 FFT) already released — data confirmed the stagflation dilemma. BOE Pill: 'I stand ready to act if necessary to contain lasting inflationary pressures.' Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: UK Composite PMI 51.0 (miss vs 52.8); 'war stalling growth, driving inflation'
GBP/USD below 200 DMA (1.3434); traded 1.3365–1.3445
UK CPI released 07:00 FFT — stagflation dilemma confirmed
BOE Pill: 'stand ready to act to contain lasting inflationary pressures'
Manufacturing resilience (51.4 beat) vs weak services (51.2 miss vs 53.0 exp)

JPY.tone: USD Rebound Pushes USD/JPY Higher — Soft CPI Temporary
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. USD/JPY firmed to 158.27–158.79 as the dollar rebounded and risk-on environment reduced haven demand. Japan's National Core CPI decelerated to 1.6% (exp 1.7%) and Headline CPI to 1.3% (exp 1.5%), but ING noted 'the soft print is likely temporary and does not alter the BOJ's tightening path.' BOJ minutes from January (pre-war) showed members monitoring economic conditions closely. Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: USD/JPY firmed to 158.27–158.79 on USD rebound and risk-on environment
Japan Core CPI 1.6% (exp 1.7%); ING: 'likely temporary, doesn't alter BOJ path'
BOJ January minutes: members monitoring conditions; pre-war context limits relevance
Risk-on ceasefire narrative reduces haven demand for JPY
BOJ Core CPI at 06:00 FFT Thursday — next key catalyst for JPY

AUD.tone: CPI Miss Pulls AUD Back from 0.7000 — May Hike Odds Reduced
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today after the monthly CPI missed: 3.7% y/y (exp 3.8%), Trimmed Mean 0.2% MoM (exp 0.3%). AUD/USD pulled back from the 0.7000 resistance level. The miss reduces the urgency for a third RBA hike in May. Metals also under pressure — copper near $12,000/t, gold subdued. ASX 200 rallied on ceasefire hopes (mining stocks led by gold producers), but the FX impact of the CPI miss dominates. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: AUD CPI 3.7% y/y (miss vs 3.8% exp); Trimmed Mean 0.2% MoM (miss vs 0.3%)
AUD/USD pulled back from 0.7000 resistance — May hike odds reduced
Copper near $12,000/t; gold subdued amid firmer USD and conflicting geopolitics
ASX 200 rallied on ceasefire hopes (mining stocks) but FX impact of CPI miss dominates
No major AUD events remaining this week — trades on risk sentiment and oil

CAD.tone: Oil Floor Holding — Ceasefire Hopes Cap But Attacks Keep Bid
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. Oil is rangebound but the floor is holding: WTI $88.50–$92.57/bbl, Brent $100.21–$104.35/bbl. Ceasefire hopes cap the upside but ongoing attacks on Isfahan nuclear sites and US troop deployment to the Middle East keep the energy risk premium intact. Macquarie forecasts Brent floor at $85–$90/bbl if tensions decrease. CAD trades as a pure petro-proxy today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Oil floor holding: WTI $88.50–$92.57/bbl, Brent $100.21–$104.35/bbl
Ceasefire hopes cap upside; Isfahan nuclear site attacks and US troops keep floor
Macquarie: Brent floor $85–$90/bbl if tensions ease; $150/bbl if Hormuz stays shut
Iran charging ships for Hormuz passage — supply disruption risk premium intact
No major CAD events today — pure petro-proxy

CHF.tone: SNB Intervention Resistance Confirmed — Appreciation Pressure Capped
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. SNB Chairman Schlegel confirmed elevated intervention readiness: 'CHF is a safe haven in uncertain times; conflict increases appreciation pressure; SNB's readiness to intervene is elevated.' EUR/CHF stabilized after the SNB's Thursday intervention signal. The Franc retains structural haven characteristics but official resistance is now too explicit to ignore. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: SNB Schlegel: 'readiness to intervene is elevated; FX interventions more suitable in some situations'
CHF appreciated 2.5% trade-weighted since December — SNB sees this as excessive
EUR/CHF stabilizing after SNB intervention signal; haven bid officially capped
SNB Quarterly Bulletin today — key event for CHF direction
Ceasefire hopes reduce haven demand, reinforcing bearish CHF case

NZD.tone: Underperforming Across G10 — Westpac Flags Q2 GDP Contraction
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. AUD and NZD underperformed across G10 in overnight trade. Westpac flagged that New Zealand GDP is set to contract in Q2 due to rising fuel prices. RBNZ Chief Economist Conway: 'monetary policy can't solve the cost-of-living crisis alone; structural reforms needed.' No domestic hawkish catalyst. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: NZD underperformed across G10 overnight alongside AUD
Westpac: 'NZD GDP set to contract in Q2 due to rising fuel prices'
RBNZ Conway: 'monetary policy can't solve cost-of-living crisis alone'
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent
High-beta risk proxy with no domestic hawkish catalyst
